  3992→Sounds absurd, no? We have all heard about Finance for Non Finance, that is providing Engineers (ie. Non Fianance) basic ideas about Finance. No denying the fact that Finance for Non Finance is very much required because engineers and all others not from the Finance fraternity needs to get it clear that as a part of organisation, that an organisations runs and flow with money, the business with which an organisation is engaged is all about money in terms of revenues, profits and cash flows. Therefore Non Finance individuals must be acquanted with the basics of Finance.
  3993→
  3994→But what about Non Finance (ie. concepts or basics of the engineering or the business that the organisation is engaged with) for Finance (ie. for Chartered/Cost Accountants, Finance MBAs, etc)
  3995→
  3996→Just like Engineers must have basics of Finance because organisation exists for the generation of money which engineers must understand, Similarly, Finance personnel must have the basics of the business they are in because organisation executes the business because of which the money is generated. So it is vice versa. With an understanding of the business, the Finance personnel gets a vivid idea about the profitability of the business, why advances and to what extent is required for such project business to run, requirement of bank guarantees and cah flow, etc. They would appreciate the requirements of all the aspects of Finance after knowing what the business is like and how it needs to be supported. Project execution is the “medium” to generate money. Project is the cause and Money is the effect. Until and unless Finance personnel appreciates the Cause-Effect mechanism, they will be operating in their own kingdom and in the process a project may be hampered due to certain Finance decision taken for the project.
  3997→
  3998→Therefore engineers and managers and Finance personnel must work in tandem with an underatnding of each other’s aspect and dynamics so that the organisation is successful.  
  3999→
  4000→Corporates must incorporate “Non Finance for Finance” training programs apart from the regular stereo typed “Finance for Non Finance” programs. I think understanding of business between the Finance and Non Finance people will increase substantially leading to coopertaion, unity and harmony during project execution.
  4001→
  4002→**CHAPTER : GROUP ANTHEM AND GROUP DRESS**
  4003→
  4004→I feel proud when the Army or the Navy or the Air Force wears their designated dress. They surely feel proud to wear that dress and surely always feel proud to be a part of that force.
  4005→
  4006→Similarly, when we work in an organization, it is our duty to work for the organization and more importantly should feel proud to be a part of the organization. So why don’t we have a company dress in the organization. Is it a bizarre idea? I am not sure. But I have seen employees in factories wearing their company dresses. Then why not in offices?
  4007→
  4008→Similarly we surely feel patriotic when we sing the National Anthem. Then why not have a company anthem? Maybe it can boost the morale and unity amongst employees and foster harmony in the organization. The Anthem can be sung before every important occasion in the organization.
  4009→
  4010→**CHAPTER : UNIFORM PAY STRUCTURE, POSITIONS AND RULES**
  4011→
  4012→HR frames rules and regulations for an organization. All employees in an organization need to follow these rules in letter and spirit. There are rules framed for Attendance, Dress code, Leaves, Benefits and facilities extended, Local, Domestic and Foreign tours, etc. for each Staff/Managerial position.
  4013→
  4014→Now, for example, the Tata’s, Birla’s, Goenka’s, Reliance’s, Adani’s, Mahindra’s, etc have lot of group companies. My point is why not the parent company of each group frames similar rules for its group companies. 
  4015→
  4016→Let me explain what I mean. The HR framework across all group companies can be the same. Its understandable that the strength and volumes of business of different companies of a parent company can differ widely. Therefore the framework can remain the same; however, the data can be tweaked to address any group company appropriately.
  4017→
  4018→Let me give examples. Suppose a parent company frames different grades of Managerial positions from M1 to M20; M1 having the least salary structure and M20 having the highest salary structure. Now the M1 grade salary may not be appropriate for a large group company whose turnover and profits are enormous. So such a company can incorporate managerial grades from M5 to M15, as you feel appropriate. Another small group company can adopt Managerial grades from M1 to M8, say as it suits them. And so on and so forth. In this system, the framework of the Managerial grades in different group companies can remain the same.
  4019→
  4020→Similarly, the concept can be extended to the positions associated with each managerial grade. However, framework for positions can be a challenge because a small sized group company will also be eager to keep a position like the Vice President (feel good factor) just as a large sized group company would also like to do so. But you can easily imagine that there is a lot of difference in the status and strength of a Vice President of a large sized company versus the Vice President of a small sized company. However, at least, the framework for the Managerial grades can be kept the same.
  4021→
  4022→Now, in the Managerial grades from M1 to M20, the rules pertaining to Leaves, Benefits and facilities extended, Local, Domestic and Foreign travels, etc can also be tagged to each Managerial grade with different applicable data. Some facility like Foreign Travel may not be applicable to a particular or range of managerial grades.
  4023→
  4024→The above concept can also be extended to the Staff grade.
  4025→
  4026→There may be challenges in adopting such a common framework of grades across group companies but the advantage of it is its uniformity. After all, every option has pros and cons; still we need to adopt a particular option in life if the positives of the option weigh more.  
  4027→
  4028→**ANNEXURES**
  4029→

4028→**ANNEXURES**
  4029→
  4030→**PART 28 : ANNEXURES**
  4031→
  4032→Annexures deals with subjects which are sometimes wrongly interpreted or less understood. Therefore I thought to discuss on some subjects in this Part to provide clarity to young professionals. However, you can delve on these subjects in various documents for a deeper understanding.
  4033→
  4034→**ANNEXURE: VISION & MISSION**
  4035→
  4036→Many employees are not clear what a Mission or a Vision of an organization is. Let us try to understand.
  4037→
  4038→Every organisation has a Mission and a Vision and they emphasize this to its employees and the outside world through a statement called the Vision and Mission statement.
  4039→
  4040→A mission statement defines the organization's business, its objectives, and how it will reach these objectives. A vision statement details where the organization aspires to go.
  4041→
  4042→Sometimes the terms “mission statement” and “vision statement” are used interchangeably or even combined into a single statement.  
  4043→But they mean two very different things. Your mission statement is what your company is doing right now, while your vision statement is what you hope to achieve in the future – where you are in this moment versus where you’re going.
  4044→
  4045→Let me give a practical example. If someone asks you, “So, what do you do currently?” you might say, “I’m a Bidding Manager in a large-sized construction company”. But, what if they asked you, “What do you want to be in five or 10 years from now?” Your answer might be a bit different, right? Maybe you’d say, “My goal is to become the Strategic Business Head and ultimately would like to be the Managing Director of the organisation”
  4046→
  4047→In the above example, as a Bidding Manager, your Mission is to win many bids and have the largest market share but your Vision may be to head the business and eventually become the MD of the organisation.
  4048→
  4049→**ANNEXURE: DPR TO CONTRACTS CLOSURE**
  4050→
  4051→There are various stages of a project executed by a customer through its selected contractor. Let us understand the entire process step by step.
  4052→
  4053→1\. **DPR** \- Every customer studies in details the benefits of a project when implemented. It studies the requirements to build the project, ie. Land, cost of acquisition, permissions from different authorities, finance, manpower and equipment infrastructure and the timelines of construction. A Detail Project Report (DPR) is prepared comprising of above details which is subsequently approved by concerned authority.
  4054→
  4055→2\. **NIT** \- The customer publishes in brief, in newspapers, websites, etc., about the requirement of the project and invites prospective contractors to participate in the bidding for the project. This is called the Notice Inviting Tender (NIT) which also mentions the last date of procuring the bidding documents.
  4056→
  4057→3\. **Bid** \- Once the NIT is published, interested contractors purchase the bidding documents and prepare the bid. The last date of submission of the bid is also mentioned in the bidding documents. The bid generally has two parts, one is the Techno-commercial bid, which comprises of all technical and commercial documents required to be submitted in the bid, and the other is the Price or Financial bid which comprises of all Price Schedules detailing item wise and total prices with a Summary of Price Schedules. The compilation of all these documents is sealed in packets and submitted to the customer within the due date of submission is called the Bid. A bid needs to be valid for a period of time, could be three to six months, as prescribed. This means the bidder cannot change its prices or documents during this bid validity period.
  4058→
  4059→A bid has to be accompanied by a Bank Guarantee called the bid guarantee. This is a legal instrument for the customer which can be forfeited in the bank of the bidder in case (1) the bidder does not accept the bid corrections (as is discussed below) (2) the bidder withdraws its bid during the bid validity period. In such cases of withdrawal of bids, the bidder may be black listed for participating in future bids of the customer.
  4060→
  4061→4\. **Bid opening** – Once bids of various bidders are submitted, the opening of the bids can be on a single part or on a two part basis. In a single part bid, both the Prequalification bid and Price bid of all bidders are opened in front of representatives of all bidders and brief particulars submitted in the bids are announced including the Total Bid Price quoted. In a two part bid, only the Techno-commercial bid of all bidders are opened and brief particulars submitted in the bids are announced.
  4062→
  4063→5\. **Bid evaluation** – Evaluation of the bids is undertaken. Firstly, evaluation of Techno-Commercial Bids is undertaken to scrutiny if the submissions are in line with the requirements of the bidding documents. Bids which meet these requirements are called responsive Techno-commercial bids. Bidders submitting non responsive Techno-commercial bids are rejected and their Price bids are not evaluated though being announced on the bid opening date.
  4064→
  4065→Thereafter, in a single part bid, the Price bids are scrutinised for responsiveness for responsive Techno-commercial bidders only. Bidders submitting non responsive Price bids are rejected. Amongst the responsive Price bids of responsive Techno-commercial bidders, the least priced Bidder is declared as L1, the next L2 and so on. In a two part bid, the bidders who submitted responsive Techno-commercial bids are notified for the opening of their Price bids on a particular date and thereafter the process of Price bid evaluation and finalisation of L1, L2, etc. is undertaken in a similar manner as described above.
  4066→
  4067→A important note on Bid evaluation criteria. Most bids in India are based on least priced bids as described above. For a bid to be techno-commercial responsive, you just need to meet the minimum requirements stipulated in the specifications. All bidders who are responsive techno-commercially are treated equally. And then their Price bids are evaluated.
  4068→
  4069→However, in more stringent bids, many such in the international domain, the evaluation stipulates weightage of each bid. For example, a Techno-commercial bid may have a 60% weightage and the Price bid may have 40% weightage. In such a case, apart from meeting minimum requirements, the bidder whose technical and commercial particulars submitted are much of higher standards than others are rewarded by getting more points due to the high weightage. Even if the bid is not the least priced tender, the bidder may get the award if it scores high in the techno-commercial bid. **Such awards are not based on least priced bids.**
  4070→
  4071→6\. **Bid clarification** \- Small errors in the Techno-commercial bid and the Price bid can be corrected by the customer and notify the bidder for their acceptance. The customer can also seek for missing documents from the bidder. If a bidder cannot provide missing documents or do not accept to the logical corrections in the Price bid, the customer has the right to reject such bidders.
  4072→
  4073→6\. **Pre-award session** – The L1 bidder is invited for a pre-award session where the L1 bidder is asked to re-assure fulfilling the important conditions of the bidding documents. The contractor also confirms to provide required bank guarantees upon award and jointly with the customer draw up a item wise time lines of project execution. This broad timeline drawn in line with completion period of the project is called the L1 network. All these discussions are recorded to produce a Minutes of Meeting (MoU) signed off by both the customer and the L1 bidder.
  4074→
  4075→7\. **Letter of Intent (LoI**) – This step is not mandatory. Sometimes the customer proceeds directly to the next step. However, in some cases, the customer informs the L1 bidder of its intent to award the project. This is called the LoI which generally mentions the total contract price, if awarded.
  4076→
  4077→8\. **Notification of Award (NOA)** – The L1 bidder is invited and the customer places the award through a letter called the Notification of Award (NOA). NOA may also be called a Award, Order or a Contract. It is at this stage that a bid is called a Contract and the L1 bidder is now known as the Contractor. The NOA also comprises of the bidding documents, the Techno-commercial bid and the Price Bid, and the MoU of pre-award session.
  4078→
  4079→9\. **Project Execution** – Based on the terms and conditions laid out in the Contract, the project is executed by the contractor. On the basis of the L1 network, the contractor draws up a slightly detailed item wise timelines called the L2 network. The L2 network is discussed and approved by the customer. In some cases, further minutely detailed networks such as the L3, L4 and L5 networks are also drawn up.
  4080→
  4081→10\. **Project Completion** – Based on the detailed networks, the project is completed physically and handed over to the customer for pre-commissioning tests.
  4082→
  4083→11\. **Pre-commissioning** – Pre-commissioning means operational tests which are conducted to ensure successful operation of the project.
  4084→
  4085→12\. **Commissioning** – The successful pre-commissioned project now commences operations.
  4086→
  4087→13\. **Contracts closure** – It includes reconciliation of materials to be supplied to the customer including spare parts as per Price Schedules and contract vis-a-vis actually supplied. Similarly, it also includes reconciliation of finances, payment to receive from customer as per Price Schedules and actual payments received. The differences are noted and accordingly transactions between the customer and the contractor are undertaken to nullify the differences.
  4088→
  4089→13\. **DLP** – Defect Liability Period (DLP) is typically a period of 12 months from the date of physical completion of the project or 18 months from the last supplied made to the project, whichever is later, during which the contractor is liable to rectify all defects pertaining to supply and services rendered during the execution of the project. Such rectifications have to be undertaken by the contractor at it’s own cost. Even if the supplies or services were accepted by the customer during project execution, the contractors must rectify the same during DLP if similar or other defects arise in the same supplies or services.
  4090→
  4091→A short note on Latent Defects Period (LDP) versus DLP. Sometimes, an inherent defect does not show up within a DLP of one year due to technical reasons. For example, a roof of a building may have been cast with inferior materials or services and the leakage during monsoon shows up after a period of three years. Such inherent defects, though not initially visible or show up in a DLP of one year, are called Latent Defects. Some clients also put a clause of LDP of 10 years besides stipulating DLP of one year. In a LDP too, the contractor must rectify these latent defects even if similar or other defects did not arise during DLP. Such latent defects must be reactivated by the contractor at it’s own cost. Due to such LDP clause, contractors have to ensure very good quality of construction; otherwise the contractor may have to incur losses in the long run.
  4092→
  4093→**ANNEXURE: JOINT VENTURE PARTNERSHIPS AND PRE-BID TIE UPS**
  4094→
  4095→If you have read the Annexure on “DPR to Contracts Closure”, you know how a customer evaluates the Techno-commercial bid of bidders. You need to meet the minimum techno-commercial requirements specified in the bidding documents. **This is called the Pre-Qualification criteria, PQ criteria i**n short.
  4096→
  4097→PQ criteria stipulate requirements in different areas. The bidder may be required to have completed a minimum number similar projects in the past (Experience based PQ), to have manufactured a minimum quantity of a material or product (Manufacturing based PQ), to possess a minimum number of a particular construction equipment (Equipment based PQ), to have a Minimum Average Annual Turnover (MATT) and a minimum amount of Liquid Assets certified by a Chartered Accountant (Finance based PQ), to have a minimum number of qualified engineers in different disciplines to be posted in the project in case of an award (HR based PQ)
  4098→
  4099→Now the issue is a bidder may satisfy one PQ but not the other or some parts of different PQ. Therefore he plans to rope in a partner where the partner can fulfill those PQ or parts of PQ which the bidder fails to fulfill. Such an arrangement is called a Joint Venture (JV). The bidders, two or more in number, are called the JV partners. The JV is committed through a common understanding which forms the basis of an agreement called the JV Agreement. In a JV agreement, the details include the different scope of work of the JV partners and other terms and conditions by which all JV partners shall abide. The bid submitted by the JV is called a JV bid which must also include the JV Agreement to be scrutinised by the customer.
  4100→
  4101→JV bids are usually allowed by customers. However please note that the customer can put riders on different PQ such as, each JV partner must fulfill at least 25% of Experience based PQ, 50% of Finance based PQ and so on. In some other kinds of PQ like HR based PQ, there may be no riders.
  4102→
  4103→Searching for the JV partner is a tough exercise of the Marketing Department. It not only has to ensure that the partner fulfills the required PQ which it cannot fulfill on its own as well as that the partner so selected is competitive. Fulfilling both the objectives which are mandatory to win a contract is really a difficult job since such partners may not be found in plenty in the market. Also partners who joined hands with other bidders in the past sometimes form a “permanent” kind of association and therefore such partners are unlikely to join hands with a new bidder until that partner wishes to opt out from its earlier arrangement.
  4104→
  4105→Many JV partnerships fail across the globe. Mistrust and misunderstanding crop up which are the reasons of partnerships being broken. Trusting and creating a win-win situation while drafting JV Agreements satisfying all partners is a necessity for a JV to sustain. A successful JV stands a long way for the success of organisations in the long run since the same partners can hold hand in hand and win many future contracts.
  4106→
  4107→**Exclusive Pre-bid tie ups**  
  4108→Exclusive partnerships with a particular vendor for a selected time period or for a particular bid are called a pre-bid tie up. In an exclusive pre-bid tie up, the vendor is not allowed to give any kind of offers to other competitors for the stated time period or the bid and the contractor too, on award, cannot place an order to any other similar vendor other than the pre-bid tie up vendor. 
  4109→
  4110→Therefore a Pre-bid tie up works for a vendor if it thinks that the contractor has a high chance of winning and therefore the vendor offers it’s best possible prices to the contractor. 
  4111→
  4112→Such a pre-bid tie up also works well for a contractor if it thinks that it has high chance of winning a bid by receiving the best possible prices from the vendor.
  4113→
  4114→Without a pre-bid tie up, a vendor does not generally offer best prices because it keeps its price escalated to keep a cushion for future negotiations. The contractor knows this and therefore in a pre-bid tie up it knows that the vendor have already released all its negotiation margins and have offered the best possible prices. Thus on award, generally, the contractor has no room to negotiate with the vendor further. Such a clause may already be stipulated in the pre-bid agreement between the contractor and the vendor.  
  4115→
  4116→**ANNEXURE: LIQUIDATED DAMAGES & CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES**
  4117→
  4118→Liquidated damages are defined as a specified amount of money to be compensated for a breach of contract. The clause could be as follows:
  4119→
  4120→Liquidated damages (LD) shall be 0.1% of contract value per week of delay or PART THEREOF subject to a maximum of 5% of contract value. In other words, if a contractor delays a project by some period of time due to no fault of the client, then 0.1% of contract value shall be deducted per week of delay from the contractor. 
  4121→
  4122→Two important points are to be noted; 
  4123→
  4124→1) Note the phrase PART THERFOF, which means even a part of the week. Most employees get this wrong. If I asked what will be the damages for 10 days of delay, many answers 0.1% x 10/7 \= 0.14%, ie. They follow the unitary method. But it is not so because 10 days of delay will be considered as 2 weeks of delay, hence the liquidated damages shall be 2 x 0.1% \= 0.2%  
  4125→2) The deduction shall be on contract price, which may be a huge sum, until some contract mentions damages to be considered on balance unexecuted portion of the contract value. Therefore this is an important point of concern. 
  4126→
  4127→Consequential damages are a very dangerous clause. That is, if there is a delay of the contractor, then the client shall default in its service to its customer for which the customer can levy a huge penalty on the client. This penalty will be passed on by the client to the contractor which is called the consequential damages. Such damages may wipe off your entire profits of the project and that is why this is a dangerous clause to accept.   
  4128→ 
  4129→
  4130→**ANNEXURE: PART OF BID SUBMISSIONS**
  4131→
  4132→Some employees get confused on part bid submissions. Bids comprises of two parts; the Techno-Commercial bid containing documents of all technical and commercial requirements and Price bid containing the Price Schedules and the Summary Price.
  4133→
  4134→Two part single opening may follow two methods; (1) both the parts will be opened in one go, thereafter client will take time to evaluate responsiveness of techno-commercial bid and thereafter prices will be evaluated for only the responsive techno-commercial bids and eventually declare the position of the bids, L1, L2 and so on based on price bid responsiveness (2)  both the parts will be opened in one go, the prices will be announced; thereafter client will take time to evaluate responsiveness of techno-commercial bid and subsequently prices will be evaluated for only the responsive techno-commercial bids and eventually declare the position of the bids, L1, L2 and so on based on price bid responsiveness. So you may have been declared L1 but eventually you may not be the evaluated L1.
  4135→
  4136→Two part two opening means only the techno-commercial bid will be opened first and then the client will take time to evaluate responsiveness of techno-commercial bid, thereafter the Price bids shall be opened on another date for only responsive Techno-commercial bids and these price bids will be evaluated eventually declare the position of the bids, L1, L2 and so on based on price bid responsiveness. In this case, the bidder cannot modify their price bid since it remains submitted to the client right at the beginning.
  4137→
  4138→In both cases, non-responsive techno-commercial bids and non-responsive price bids are rejected and are not considered for award. In such cases the bid guarantees submitted by the bidder are returned to the customer in due course of time. 
  4139→
  4140→Another important point to be noted on the forfeiture of bid security. Bid security can be forfeited under the following circumstances;
  4141→
  4142→1) If the bidder withdraws its bid during the bid validity period  
  4143→2) If the bidder does not accept the corrections made by the client on its techno-commercial bid and price bid where mistakes have crept in  
  4144→3) If the bidder does not accept award from the client based on its submitted techno-commercial and price bid.  
  4145→4) If the bidder does not accept client’s request to withdraw the deviations provided by the bidder
  4146→
  4147→Also please note on an important cross breach clause. Sometimes a client splits an award to a contractor in two parts; the Techno-commercial contract and the Price contract. In such cases, breach of any contract will be automatically deemed to be a breach on the other contract. This is known as the cross breach clause.
  4148→
  4149→**ANNEXURE: DEFECTS LIABILITY PERIOD AND LATENT DEFECTS PERIOD**
  4150→
  4151→Many employees are not conversant with the concept of Latent Defect. Defects Liability Period (DLP) is the period after a contract is commissioned during which the contractor has to provide warranty to remedy defects or replace the defective goods or services. Typically DLP is 12 months from the commissioning of the contract or 18 months from the last supplies made in the contract, whichever is later.
  4152→
  4153→Latent Defects Period (LDP) is the period after a contract is commissioned during which the contractor has to provide warranty to remedy or replace defects which show up after considerable period of time. An example of this is poor quality casting of roof of a building in which case seepage may show up after several years of withering monsoons. Therefore these defects are those which are inherently present but do not show up during the DLP. Typically LDP may be 10 years from the commissioning of the contract. 
  4154→
  4155→**ANNEXURE: BID PROCESS FLOW**
  4156→
  4157→A Bid Manager may keep a track of various ongoing bid preparations by following systematically and noting the progress as follows:
  4158→
  4159→Due Dates for
  4160→
  4161→1) Purchase of biding documents  
  4162→2) Seeking clarifications  
  4163→3) Submission of bid
  4164→
  4165→Document purchase process
  4166→
  4167→4) Study of NIT  
  4168→5) PQ (as mentioned in NIT) study before purchase of document  
  4169→6) Management approval for purchase of documents  
  4170→7) Preparation of Demand Draft and purchase of bidding documents
  4171→
  4172→PQ study process
  4173→
  4174→8) Study of PQ  
  4175→9) Discussion and action plan to meet PQ  
  4176→10) Arrange PQ documents
  4177→

4420→
  4421→Many organisations recruit employees on a Contractual basis, say for a particular project just awarded to the company. He feels uncomfortable that since the position is not permanent he may be removed from the company at any point of time or when the project is completed if there are no more projects to serve.
  4422→
  4423→However, such a thought has a flaw. Today, every permanent employee have in its employment terms and conditions that either the company can remove you with one months’ notice or you may resign from the company by providing one months’ notice without citing any reasons. Therefore, for whatever reasons, if the company wants to remove you at any point of time or after the completion of a project, the company can do so without any obligations or providing reasons. So, what was the use of being permanent? Whether permanent or contractual, company can remove any employee in line with the employment terms.
  4424→
  4425→**CHAPTER : EPC & PPP**
  4426→
  4427→**PART 29: PERSONAL ADVICE**
  4428→
  4429→I feel that those who consider these advices will go a long way in their corporate lives. These advices are entirely personal and therefore I leave it to your wisdom whether to consider or not.
  4430→
  4431→**ATTITUDE**
  4432→
  4433→Assume A is designated as 1, B as 2, C as 3 and so on till Z as 26\.  
  4434→Now add the numbers against each character of ATTITUDE, ie. 1(A)+20(T)+20(T)+9(I)+20(T)+21(U)+4(D)+5(E) \= 100
  4435→
  4436→Probably ATTITUDE is the only English word whose designated numeric numbers add to 100\. This is just a coincidence, however the number 100 attached to the word ATTITUDE signifies immense importance.
  4437→
  4438→You will observe that some people may have the right vision, knowledge and skills but lacks attitude to accomplish a certain work, be it for any reason. The right attitude steers your knowledge and skills into action thereby accomplishing the work in hand. If you do not have the right attitude, you cannot succeed in life however knowledgeable you may be and however great skills you may possess. 
  4439→
  4440→**Have the correct attitude always; it will pay rich dividends in your life.**
  4441→
  4442→**BE HUMANE**
  4443→
  4444→When a child grows up, relatives and neighbours often ask what you would like to be when you grow up. Some say doctor, some say engineer, and some even say pilot. But I taught my child, which I suppose every parent should, that when asked about his future, he should say that he wants to be a good human being apart from anything else that someone wants to be. Being a good human being goes a long way in your life, even in the corporate world as you may have heard about Sir Ratan Tata. Good human beings can manage and deal with people better in the corporate. This is an advantage to them apart from the vision, knowledge and skills they possess. 
  4445→
  4446→**PEOPLE MANAGEMENT**
  4447→
  4448→I still remember one of the wise words that my boss taught when I stepped into the Marketing domain in my earlier career. “Listen Abhik, while you build your career in Marketing, you will come across various kind of people; good and bad, honest and dishonest, polite and angry, knowledgeable and not knowledgeable, helpful and not so helpful, co-operative and jealous, kind and unkind, biased and unbiased, etc., so you must (1) learn how to deal with all these kind of people because you cannot simply ignore their presence in the organization and (2) learn how to say “No’ with a smile because you cannot say yes to proposals and statements that you feel are not right.
  4449→
  4450→In fact when I assumed the position of Marketing Head and stayed on for ten years and eventually became the Business Head and stayed on for the last three years of my career, I found that managing people was the most difficult job for me. As an organization, I encountered many technical and financial challenges but I had the support of expertise in all the fields of operation, be it in Designs, Projects, Supply Chain, Quality, Safety or Finance or any other department whatsoever to overcome these challenges. But Heads and senior people often had different opinions, sometimes judgmental, contradictory and sometimes steered by vested interest, jealousy and anger. So hearing out everyone, using my vision and knowledge and taking a final decision was often very challenging because the final decision did not adhere to some of the opinions yet I had to convince all as to why the final decision has been taken.   
  4451→Every human being has an ego which they wish to be satisfied. You cannot ignore people who work with you, yet you need to take decisions which are not in line with some of the opinions. 
  4452→
  4453→People are very sensitive. All their opinions must be heard to. At times, their opinions may be disregarded and at times theirs could be the best option. You have to treat all your colleagues humanly with due respect. And you must have the right attitude and power to convince all the reasons for your final decision.  
  4454→   
  4455→Hence I found man management the most difficult job in my career as well as the most interesting and challenging. 
  4456→
  4457→**Develop man management skills to succeed in life.**
  4458→
  4459→**FIRST OPPORTUNITY**
  4460→
  4461→Opportunity comes and goes. Sometimes it comes only once. You should grab opportunity at the first time itself, provided it’s reasonably good, lest a better one does not come later. You have to hold your greed and grab a reasonable opportunity at the first instance itself because most of the times a better opportunity does not come again.
  4462→
  4463→Once in my early part of my career, I was selected for a management course at the Imperial Institute of Management in UK by the company. I was reluctant because I was comfortable in my job at Kolkata and I thought to undergo the course after a year or two and till then to earn some money. I due course of time, the sponsorship was stopped by the company and that opportunity never came again. Eventually, much later on, I had to settle for a Advanced Diploma Course in Management from the Institute of Management Technology (IIMT) through a distance learning mode. 
  4464→
  4465→Once in a Maruti True Value showroom, when I did not have enough money to buy a new car, I spotted a car which I wished to buy since the price was very reasonable. I kept it on my buy list. In greed to get a better deal, I visited another showroom but could not get a deal as good as the earlier one. I rushed to the first showroom again to seal the deal but the car was sold by then. Eventually I purchased a car but at a higher price.
  4466→
  4467→Most of the times I have observed that the first opportunity I got were the best opportunity. And in case the second opportunity was better, leave the first one and take the second if possible and if not possible then don’t repent.
  4468→
  4469→**Grab the first opportunity rather than be greedy to grab a better one later**.
  4470→
  4471→**INSURANCE**
  4472→
  4473→Insurance is a cover for an individual to safeguard his interest. For example life insurance will safeguard against the livelihood costs of the nominee and his family in case of death, health insurance will safeguard against the medical costs for treatment, property insurance will safeguard against EMI payments by the nominee in case of death, house insurance will safeguard against the costs for damage of house due to calamities, cyber insurance will safeguard against cyber frauds and threats.
  4474→
  4475→You have to pay premiums for taking such insurances. But many wise people argue foolishly at the end of a year that they have been profitable by not taking insurance cover and thus paying premiums since nothing happened to them in the year. Financially it may be so but technically not. No one desires that something bad happens to them ever; however, one should think that you need to de-risk such undesirable events. Hence is the importance of insurance. All individuals are advised to take insurances. 
  4476→
  4477→**Insurance is a cover for risk; the premium is for attaining peace of mind.**  
  4478→       
  4479→**HEALTH**
  4480→
  4481→Health is wealth. We understand it better as we age and we sometimes find ourselves in unhealthy conditions or become sick. While we are young, being sick is rare and so we then think Money is Wealth, not Health. However, today many youngsters are prone to sickness due to many factors, personal and environmental.
  4482→
  4483→As we remain busy in our corporate life, health, the most precious asset that one possess, is often ignored. Continuous such ignorance may lead to health complications later on. One must sincerely take care of his health while on service. Do not postpone it till retirement. Maintain health right from day one.
  4484→
  4485→Health pertains to different domains of an individual;  
  4486→Physical health is about taking care of your body;  
  4487→Mental health is about taking care of your mind;  
  4488→Spiritual health is about taking care of your soul;
  4489→
  4490→Body, mind and soul are all interrelated and define your wellbeing. Your satisfactory wellbeing is a catalyst to a good personal or corporate life. If your body or the mind or the soul or any combination thereof is disturbed, your personal life and corporate life too will be disturbed.
  4491→
  4492→Health pertaining to other domains of an individual is:  
  4493→Financial health is about managing your finances and assets  
  4494→Sexual health is about taking interest in your sex life  
  4495→Social health is about your involvement in the society  
  4496→Job health is about your satisfaction in your job or business
  4497→
  4498→Above health parameters too affect your wellbeing. For example, if your financial health is poor, you will be anxious and will hamper your mental health. And if your mental health is poor, your physical health will be affected. All are interrelated.
  4499→
  4500→Also, don’t miss your annual or bi-annual basic health check-ups comprising of blood tests, Chest x-ray, USG and ECG. It is interesting to note that many people think they are fine and healthy but health check-ups reveal that certain health parameters are beyond normal values which require immediate treatment. What I am trying to sensitise that many such illness develop within our bodies SILENTLY.  For example, thousands of people do not know that they are pre-diabetic or have high blood pressure because they don’t check. These are silent killers. So please don’t miss your health check-ups.    
  4501→**Health is wealth. It is one of the most precious assets that one possesses. Please don’t ignore**  
  4502→   
  4503→**PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT**
  4504→
  4505→This is again a ignored subject while we are in service. Someone comes and convinces of investing in a FD, we do it. Someone comes and asks to invest in ULIPs, we do it. Someone else comes and convinces us that there is lot of money to be earned in the stock market, we invest in the equities. Someone says to buy a flat instead of paying rents, we do so. That someone could be you yourself. You may think to put some money in gold, you do it. Whatever someone tries to convince or we are convinced about investment, we do it. However, all these investments are done in a haphazard manner.
  4506→
  4507→Here comes the issue of portfolio management. There is no harm in investing money in different class of assets, but it has to be done in a scientific manner. Firstly, the class of assets can be classified into equity, debt, property and metal. The percentage of investment into these different class could be based on one's age (the less the age, the more may be invested in equities), risk taking power (the less the risk taking power is, the more may be invested in debts), interest (if on a transferable job, someone may not be interested in property). 
  4508→
  4509→A typical mid aged person with moderate risk taking power can invest like;  
  4510→35% on equity  
  4511→25% on debt  
  4512→25% on property  
  4513→15% on metal
  4514→
  4515→Equity could be the stock market or equity MFs. Debt could be bank FD, corporate FD and corporate bonds. Property could be residential or commercial. Metal could be physical gold or silver, paper gold or silver, platinum or copper, zinc and aluminum in the MCX stock exchange. 
  4516→
  4517→Please take the help of a portfolio manager, who could be your Finance guy himself, to guide you for a good portfolio.
  4518→
  4519→**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**

4519→**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**
  4520→
  4521→**WORK-LIFE BALANCE**
  4522→
  4523→This is a hot debate often happening even on TV debates. How many live s have God given you? One, I suppose. Therefore you are never going to get the time lapsed of your life. Even the richest man on earth cannot buy time which has lapsed. Time gone is gone forever. And your past is also gone forever.
  4524→
  4525→So, while we have to struggle very hard in the corporates to do well in life and rise up the corporate ladder, we also need to give time for yourself (me time) and your family and friends. All is not work in life. We definitely have to work; it is a foundation to have a great life with our family and friends. So you need to balance between work and life and that is what is called the Work-Life balance. If you provide all the time to work, your relationship with your family may not be so intimate and loving, you may lose your friends and you may end up leaving all your hobbies that you so much loved to pursue.
  4526→
  4527→For a few days the balance may be upset due to pressure of work or on family matters, but on an overall basis you must strike a balance between work and life.
  4528→
  4529→**WORK-LIFE balance is about providing time to office as well as to our near and dear ones.**
  4530→
  4531→**GRATEFULNESS**
  4532→
  4533→Why do we touch the feet of our parents when we meet them? Why do we touch the feet of our beloved school teachers or our coaches? Because we are grateful to them for the care, love and teachings they have imparted to us. Without their care and support, we would not have been what we are; therefore they have been large contributors to our success in life.
  4534→
  4535→However, very unfortunately, I have seen many people not being grateful to their contributors once the contributor is no more in a position to contribute to you anymore. For example, as a business Head, you might have taught the intricacies of business to your subordinate and encouraged him and promoted him to assume higher responsibilities which he was capable of, but as you retire and you are no more in a position to give good bonus or promote him any further you are simply forgotten. It is often said that in a street or a market a clerk or a staff do not even look at a Director who have retired from his organization because the clerk knows that the Director now cannot give him any benefits further. These people are so selfish because they think why they should waste time, energy and money to talk or meet their bosses when they are useless for them\!  
  4536→
  4537→Above kind of incidences happen in every sphere of life, even within our relatives and “so called” friends. So why should someone be grateful. It has been proven scientifically they gratefulness leads to:
  4538→
  4539→1) Emotional well-being: Gratitude fosters positive emotions, reduces stress. Expressing appreciation leads to increased happiness and satisfaction of the soul  
  4540→2) Physical well-being: Gratitude improves physical health by promoting better sleep, reduces blood pressure and enhances immunity  
  4541→3) Mental well-being: Gratitude lowers depression and anxiety and helps in facing challenges in life  
  4542→4) Social connections: Gratitude strengthens relationships and reduces loneliness  
  4543→5) Contentment: Gratitude leads to greater contentment. It shifts focus from what one lacks to what one has promoting a sense of fulfillment
  4544→
  4545→**Practice gratefulness; you may not be aware how it improves your well-being**
  4546→
  4547→**WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS**
  4548→
  4549→I find it frustrating when one tries to figure out only the negative sides of a person, an option or an entity. Be it yourself, or a singer, or an actor, or a politician, or a team, or a political party, or a school, or whatever, there can be good things as well as bad things about it. Any human or agency or entity cannot be perfect in all parameters. But a good human or an agency or an entity has presumably many more good things than the bad things about them. You need to appreciate those good things and not ignore and focus on bad things. You can also weigh the importance of the good things and the bad things and come to a conclusion if the entity is good or bad.
  4550→
  4551→In our personal life and also in the corporate, we sometimes have many options to select from. You must weigh the positive and negative factors of each of the options and come to a conclusion on which option to choose. It is obvious that all options will have some negative points. There are people who are not happy with any option because he tries to see only the negative side of each option. These people cannot make proper and fast decisions needed in the corporate world. You have to choose an option with the greatest advantages amidst some disadvantages that it may have. 
  4552→
  4553→**SABJANTALOGY**
  4554→
  4555→This is the name of a subject which I coined after coming across some people who fit into this class of people. Just like Biology, Physiology, Oncology, Sociology, Anthropology, etc., Sabjantalogy deals with the science of knowing everything on earth.
  4556→
  4557→Some people may advise you on how to cook better, advise you on better medicines, teach you how to play a sports better, seemingly knows about any kind of industry on earth, knows about fabric and garments, gives you lecture on politics, or even on rocket science. The most astonishing part is that all these “knowhow??” has been formed on his own whims and fancies and not formed by reading books or learning from somewhere.
  4558→
  4559→Say for example, if you are an Arts graduate and have formed an idea about a medicine with your own experiment without consulting a doctor or learning it from any medical journal or reading about it from newspaper or elsewhere, is it right for you to prescribe the medicine to your friend? There can be hundreds of examples like this on various subjects.
  4560→
  4561→Today the world has lot of expertise in every field of life. If you really want to prescribe a medicine, ask the doctor, or if you really want to advise on how to make the best cakes, ten consult youtube or a chef. Why provide your “so called” knowledge which is actually wrong.
  4562→
  4563→**Don’t be a Sabtanjalogist; ask the specialist or learn about it.**  
  4564→
  4565→**DON’T BE A MISER**
  4566→
  4567→What is the purpose of earning money; to spend it wisely and reasonably; Isn’t it? But some accumulate money for their own satisfaction and not spend them for reasons not known to them. They are misers. Misers are of two types: universal misers and selfish misers. Universal misers are those who are reluctant to spend for anyone on earth. Selfish misers are those who are willing to spend only for him and family but not beyond.
  4568→
  4569→Spending must be wise and reasonable; you need to keep reasonable amount of money for medical emergencies, education and marriage of your children and for all mandatory purposes of life. Beyond that, you should spend for yourself, family and others for things that you love in life, be it for food, movies, clothes, gadget, tours and travels, hobbies or whatever that you think will be useful or make someone happy. Remember, there is no concept of Fixed Deposits in heaven. Everything that you earn in life has to be left. We have been born naked and will depart to Heaven naked. It is one life that God has given us. Enjoy it to the fullest extent. 
  4570→
  4571→How much to save, how much to invest and how much to spend may vary between individuals, but they have to be wisely and reasonably thought of practically. Allot money that you earn for savings, investment and expenses in a balanced practical manner.
  4572→
  4573→**Save & invest what is necessary. But spend too to enjoy and live your life.** 
  4574→
  4575→**SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD**
  4576→
  4577→There is a joke which you will hear while your child grows up. When you are asked how tall your child has grown up, you answer by gesture with your hands apart horizontally. This is because your child leaves for school early while you are asleep and the child is asleep when you come home late from office. Therefore you have not seen him grow tall vertically on office days. Suddenly you can see him how tall he is when you are at home on Sundays. 
  4578→
  4579→However, do not take it as a simple joke. Once your child is 18 years old and pass out Class XII, he may be going out of station or abroad for higher studies and subsequently for good jobs anywhere on this earth. This means you may get to stay with your child at your home only for the first 18 years of his life and thereafter you will miss him except for a few days when the child will have time to meet you at home on his holidays.
  4580→
  4581→At the same time, you will be very busy at office trying to secure your good future year after year. But amidst this rat race, do not ignore to spend as much time as possible with your child. Remember you may not get to see him for long after he reaches 18 years of age. Your good times spent with your child will remain etched in your mind forever. Do not miss these times. It will never come back again 
  4582→
  4583→**CULTIVATE A HOBBY**
  4584→
  4585→There will be times in your life when you are very sad. Maybe your boss did not give you a promotion which you thought of sure to get, or could be your fiancé decided for a break up, or incidents like these. You would not like to be besides of your friends or family at such sad moments. You would prefer to be alone for some while. So how do you spend these times alone? Cultivate a hobby which will keep you happy and busy to tide over your sad times or after your retirement. You can learn to play cards on your own; you can learn to play an instrument; you can learn to sing; you can learn to paint; or any other hobby that will give you a sense of fulfillment.
  4586→
  4587→Music was my greatest de-stressor. Playing the harmonica and the guitar lit up my soul at sad times. These instruments are still a part of my life after retirement. 
  4588→
  4589→**DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM**
  4590→
  4591→I have read it everywhere that even a peon would not like to wish or see your face on the street after you have retired as a Director or from a big position in the corporate. This may be true but not always. 
  4592→
  4593→As a leader, if you had shown good leadership by demonstrating correct attitude, being kind and supportive, managing people humanly with care and respect, being grateful to people who supported you, encouraging people to do well and rewarding them for good work and your actions on employees and all stakeholders always being unbiased and transparent and to top it all if you had been successful in your organization, then people, whoever knew you and know you presently hearing from others do remember and respect you even after you retire or even die. 
  4594→
  4595→Being unbiased even with your close colleagues and demonstrating your professionalism against personal bias at all times is seen as a great leadership. Such successful leaders are never forgotten.  
  4596→
  4597→**ENJOY LIFE**
  4598→
  4599→The Almighty have given you ONE life only. Even if your super rich, you have one life only. So always enjoy your life.
  4600→
  4601→How many hours do you have in a year? 24 hours a day x365 days a year \= 8760 hours. Assume you sleep 8 hours a day; therefore you spend 8 x 365 hours \= 2920 hours. Assume you work 10 hours a day (except Sundays and 12 annual holidays) and commute 2 hours a day to office; therefore you spend 12 x (365-52-12) hours \= 3612 hours. So you have (8760-2920-3612) hours, ie. 2228 hours a year excluding time for work and sleep. 2228 hours out of 8760 hours means around 25% of your life; could be less if you work more. Have you ever imagined that you have only 25% of your life free of work and sleep which you can utilize for yourself, family and friends and relatives? If you consider 5% time gone for maintaining your house, household works, taking your child to school and tuitions, purchase of daily groceries, etc. then you are left with 20% of your life time only. Sounds pretty less\! But that is the fact of life. So enjoy your life well with this 20% of your time. It shall also rejuvenate you to carry on with your work at office. You can do so by allotting time for:
  4602→
  4603→1) Savouring good food at home and restaurants  
  4604→2) Entertainment like hobbies, movies, fairs, gossiping with friends, etc  
  4605→3) Tours and Travels – local, out of station and international destinations
  4606→
  4607→Sorry, if I have missed anything that you would like to enjoy life it. Whatever it is, remember what Mahatma Gandhi said \- “Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever”
  4608→
  4609→**SEEING THROUGH DISRUPTORS**
  4610→
  4611→Today you will come across many disrupters during your personal and corporate life. 
  4612→
  4613→**Technological disruptors** will force you to change methods and techniques in doing your work and execute your project. As a simple example, when computers came in, typists were forced to learn software like Word, Excel, etc. to type on computers since the type writers were abolished. Any typist who did not do so lost his job.
  4614→
  4615→**Geo-political disruptors** will force you to change the way you do business. As an example the tariff episodes between USA and many other countries including India is forcing many countries to look into other avenues for their business. Such disruptors play havoc on the stock markets with their volatility.
  4616→
  4617→**Social disruptors** like break up of marriage, divorce, suicide, crimes, etc. will disturb your peace of mind which can affect your personal and corporate life. 
  4618→
  4619→**Environmental disruptors** like pollution, food adulteration, climate change, etc. is taking a toll in our health.
  4620→
  4621→In the spate of all these disruptors, you have to see through them with calm and pass them off because most are external inflicted where you have no control. It is easier said than done, but better if it can be done. You need to build a strong mind which is not easy to be disrupted. You must develop capabilities to control your mind and not allow your mind to take control of you.  
  4622→
  4623→**QUIT IF UNHAPPY**
  4624→
  4625→You can work in your company in two ways; one is with stress or pressure that is on you to perform and the other is with passion. 
  4626→
  4627→When you work with passion, you forget how long you have been working on because you work with interest not by any external force or pressure to perform. When you work for long with stress and pressure, you will undergo hormonal changes in your body harming your health. Try to find a new suitable job where you can work happily before your health deteriorates. I know finding a new job is not easy, but go on trying to save your health. Quit.  
  4628→
  4629→**CONTENTMENT IN LIFE**
  4630→
  4631→Once a middle aged officer approached me in my cabin and informed that he would like to resign because of his low pay. I was astonished to hear about the low pay and called him along with the HR manager later on. On scrutiny of his pay slip, I found that his take home pay was very less but also observed that there were lot of deductions from his salary. On deeper scrutiny I found that there were several EMIs being deducted from his salary, one for new 3BHK property, one for SUV car, one for a racing bike and another for a 75 inch LED TV. I had to only convey that the company is providing you a reasonable salary but it is due to your heavy purchases that your take home salary is meager. 
  4632→
  4633→Today many youngsters wants to have a property, car and a bike within latest 30 years of age, forget about other costly gadgets which they term them as necessary. There is no end to a materialistic world today. You must put a full stop to buying at some point of time. If you have a hatchback, you will want to have a sedan, then a SUV, then a Mercedes or a BMW, then a Ferrari, where is the end? There is no contentment in your life them, and therefore you will always remain unhappy. You need to balance your pocket. As I said earlier, you have to allot money wisely and reasonably for your savings, investment and your expenses, the total being what comes to your pocket.
  4634→
  4635→If you develop the Attitude to use money judicially and be happy with whatever you have, you will achieve a state of contentment. Contentment is about being satisfied and happy with whatever you have in life. Contentment does not look at what you do not have in life. **Achieve a state of contentment; you will be happy in life**
  4636→
  4637→**PART 30: SOME LESSONS I LEARNT**
  4638→
  4639→**The Tongue**  
  4640→What is the most important organ of human life? Obviously the heart which pumps blood more than 100,000 times a day, which means if you live for 75 years, it will pump approx. 2.75 billion times. Most miraculously, it pumps on its own; you don’t do anything for it to pump, except for taking care of it sometimes with good food and aerobic exercise. With the blood being pumped by the heart, all other organs play their vital role in their own domains.
  4641→
  4642→But what is the most important organ for living a good life? I say “the tongue”. Tongue is the only organ for the sense of taste and unhealthy but tasty food destroys our health. Food is a vital factor of our health other than some other lifestyle habits. So our tongue is responsible for our physical health. 
  4643→
  4644→Also we can speak because of our tongue. The way we speak determines our character and this character determines your relationship, be it with your family or friends or relatives or neighbours or colleagues in the corporate. The way politicians speak also determines his relationship with the public because politicians are public figures. So tongue is the main organ for your relationships. It is said that deaf and dumb do not have enemies; yes, they may have the tongue but cannot speak. 
  4645→
  4646→**Lesson**: The tongue is responsible for two major aspects in one’s life; your physical health and your relationships. Watch your tongue from now on.  
  4647→\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
  4648→
  4649→**True Friends**  
  4650→Today I am living my third life. I was returning from a seminar from an outstation place to my house. All my juniors were also returning by a bus. The seniors were returning in their own cars. The cars were ahead and the bus was following us. My driver was relatively new on the highway. He put the brakes of my car much before; almost 10 feet before a truck suddenly appeared, not realizing that a heavy 50 ton trailer was coming from behind. As soon as the car stopped, the trailer banged my car from behind and it went flying for quite a distance. The windscreen behind was shattered and the broken glass scattered inside the car. There was a spare wheel kept at the back of my car on which the trailer actually banged. Maybe the wheel absorbed most of the impact. Even if the broken glass splintered, I did not get single scratch on my forehead or eyes. I was not even injured. The driver was slightly injured. I was fine physically but shocked with trauma. I went inside the police station which was just besides the accident spot.
  4651→
  4652→All my colleagues, some them perceived friends, alighted from the bus coming from behind and came to meet me in the police station. Astonishingly I observed that almost all barring three of them consoled me but left for their homes citing important personal works. The three of them were all along with me helping me to talk to the police, file FIR, arrange a towing vehicle to take my car to the workshop and arrange another sedan car to take me home comfortably. **That day I realized who my true friends were**\!
  4653→
  4654→**Lesson**: You may have many friends flocking around you even in the corporate world. But be careful, most of them are perceived to be friends, only some are true friends. It’s possible to identify true friends at times of distress only.  
  4655→\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
  4656→
  4657→**Health is Wealth**  
  4658→As I started leading a second life, sometime later in life, I encountered chest pain occasionally. In the beginning I just thought them to be gastric pains and I presumed that the antacids were working. However, on a severe unbearable chest pain one day, I had to be hospitalized. The doctor smiled and was casual; I was annoyed a bit and asked the doctor “I had terrible chest pain and have come to consult you, but it seems you are smiling and taking it casually”. The doctor replied “my dear friend, do you know how lucky you are? Do you know how many patients come here brought dead? At least by the grace of God, you are still living and after necessary interventions you will be all right. That’s why I am smiling with a sigh of relief”. I had to undergo angioplasty on that day. **I started living a healthy life thereafter and believed Health is Wealth**
  4659→
  4660→**Lesson**: Bad times act as warnings which lead to correct your way of life. Health is Wealth   
  4661→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4662→
  4663→**Positive outlook**  
  4664→After angioplasty and taking rest for two weeks, I resumed office. Sometimes worries crept into my mind about the chest pain I had. Some relatives, deriving sadistic pleasure, even commented that these stents last for 10 years (Recall my topic on “Sabjantalogy”\!) On reaching the office, I found that preparation of celebration is on with cake cutting ceremony and a “healthy” lunch”. I asked my boss “what is this nonsense about?” He replied “Look Abhik, you had blockage and have undergone angioplasty. In fact you were lucky to have chest pain and land in the hospital. Now with the stents implanted, the blood is flowing merrily through your arteries. And your life is extended to some decades. But we live anxiously with blockages, as all humans have, and we do not know what fate lies upon us”. I really wondered about his positive thought. It gave me hope and strength to live on happily leaving behind my worries.
  4665→
  4666→**Lesson**: Live happily with hope; hope provides strength at all times  
  4667→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4668→
  4669→**Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence**  
  4670→I want to share a series of very short stories which made an impact on my life. The first one – you may have observed that a father plays with his small child by throwing him up on the air and again holding him tightly on his fall. As the child is thrown, he giggles with laughter and joy but has no fear of falling. Why? Because he knows for certain that his father is there to hold him for sure while he falls. **This is TRUST**. The second one \- Once on a hot summer afternoon, the village head asked all the villagers to gather at 10 am the next day and pray to rain god to give rains to the palace. Next day all the people, young and old, came under the scorching heat to pray to the rain god. A little young girl came with an umbrella over her head. When asked, she said it will rain after our prayers. **This is HOPE.** The third one – Sometimes when a cricketer goes to a field to bat, he looks up at the sun. Or someone prays to God before he goes to take an examination. I have also seen footballers touching the field with respect before he starts playing for a match. What are all these gestures like looking up at the sun or praying to God or touching the football field? The individuals believe that they will succeed if they resort to such acts. **This is FAITH**. The fourth one – A student studies very well for the IIT exams. He solves all the past papers. He takes coaching classes and scores very well in all the mock tests. He says he is sure to crack the exams. **This is CONFIDENCE**. 
  4671→
  4672→**Lesson**: Live and work with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence and implement your Knowledge and Skills, you are sure to succeed  
  4673→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4674→
  4675→**Be perseverant**  
  4676→When I became the Marketing Head in charge of Business Development, Marketing and Bidding in 2007, I started submitting tenders to various clients. As months passed by, I found that I was losing all the tenders and it’s a tough job. Almost 8 months passed and I lost almost 20 tenders successively. Not a single one I could win. Then one day I was on a tour and I heard that a meeting of the entire office was held by the then Business Head. On my return, I got a call from my colleague and I learnt that I was severely criticized as a Head and for my failures in the last 8 months. Not only this, The Business Head proposed to replace my position with someone better. I was very sad. I did not speak a single word about this in the office. But I wondered what wrong I am doing all these days. In the next tender, I went through the nitty-gritties of the costing, worked out every minute detail with my colleagues and submitted our price bid. The project was big and prestigious. Eventually we became L1 and were awarded the project. My Business Head changed his mind and congratulated me. I still remember my friend saying to me “Abhik, there is light at the end of each tunnel; Never lose hope”. There was no looking back again. Tenders after tenders were won and I became a prominent figure in the industry, recognized and appreciated with jealousy by our competitors too.
  4677→
  4678→In another instance, for some reason or the other, I was not getting promotion for many years though I was doing good work. Every year after the appraisals, my mother used to ask me “Did you get promotion this time”. “No” was my answer. This answer went on for consecutive years. I still worked hard. I learnt to be perseverant. I consoled myself by understanding that there may not have been enough vacancies for promotion or that some were better than me. Nothing prevented me to carry on with my hard work. I always focused on excelling on every job.  
  4679→
  4680→**Lesson**: Never lose hope; Try and try; Be perseverant; Focus on your work: You are bound to succeed  
  4681→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 
  4682→
  4683→**Humble & Caring**  
  4684→I heard from my seniors that once in Bombay House, the corporate office of the Tata group, a new liftman was employed. In the very first day of office of the liftman armed with all the rules of using the lift, he turned away a tall gentleman who wanted to use the lift to go to first floor. The rules did not allow this and the gentleman walked up the stairs. All his floe colleagues rushed to him and said “Hey\! Are you mad? Why did you make him go up the stairs? Don’t you know who he is?” The liftman was amazed and said “No, I just followed the rules”. One of the staff then said “You are surely going to lose your job. The gentleman was Sir Ratan Tata, our Chairman”. The liftman almost lost his sense in shock. He was cursing himself. He knew he will be called sometime later and be sacked. He was very sad to have done such a mistake without knowing though he followed the rules. And after sometime, someone came down and conveyed to the liftman that the Chairman have called him. By this time the liftman was half dead. He sobbingly went upstairs and found the Chairman with his entire team assembled in the middle of the hall. He thought he was in the midst of a punishment in public. The Chairman started saying “look gentlemen; here is a man I found doing his duties very obediently and managing people on the lift irrespective of who you are. He sent me walking to this floor and did not allow me to take the lift. I appreciate his duties because he was following all the rules of using the lift”. This very same day the liftman was rewarded. Such was the greatness of Sir Ratan Tata.
  4685→
  4686→In another instance, I was watching a TV interview with the staff of Infosys. The staff was mentioning that the Chairman, Mr Narayan Murthy sometimes visits the residential block where labour resides and enquires the wellbeing of the people, whether street lights, drinking water and other amenities are in place. Such feelings for the labour staff are highly appreciated of a great person.
  4687→
  4688→In another instance of our President, Dr Abdul Kalam, who was also the Chairman of DRDO, asked one of his colleagues, who was a work alcoholic, to take his wife and children to a movie in the evening after office. The person was so busy with his work that he realized its already night and repented that he forgot to take his family to the movie. He left office and reached home with guilt on his face. He apologized to his wife for his forgetfulness. But the wife said that they had already seen the movie and come. He was amazed. He exclaimed “How”. The wife said “since you were busy in work and it was time for the movie, Dr Abdul Kalam came to our house himself and took us for the movie”. Can someone imagine the greatness of this gentleman\!
  4689→
  4690→The moral of these stories is that treat all people, even your juniors, with kindness and consideration. You do not lose your ego by being kind and considerate to your juniors, rather you demonstrate yourself as a great leader and a human being.
  4691→
  4692→**Lesson**: Be humble; Be caring; That is what a great leader is.  
  4693→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4694→
  4695→**Appreciate Performers**  
  4696→Once I started doing well as the Marketing Head winning sizeable number of bids, my MD wished that the top 10 performers of the company, which included me too, be facilitated by the then Chairman of the Tata Group, Mr Cyrus Mistry. It was a moment in my corporate life to be cherished forever. Mr Mistry came to our HO at Hyderabad and facilitated me and the others with a sincere word of appreciation. I cannot forget ever the gesture of my MD and the Chairman to have done this for me. I remain ever grateful to them. And later in life, I cried when I heard the news of Mr Mistry’s death.
  4697→
  4698→In another instance, when I was already succeeding as Marketing Head winning bids, my MD came to our office for a meeting with my bosses. The bosses accompanied the MD upstairs to our office and headed for his cabin past my own. When he saw me, he first came inside my cabin along with my bosses and almost fell on my feet.  I was shocked. I said “No No sir, please do not do that”. He said “Abhik, winning bids only allow our company to execute projects and everyone has a job to do in this company only when you win bids, so I am grateful to you and your team”. Tears rolled down my years on seeing his appreciation. I had no words to say.
  4699→
  4700→**Lesson**: Appreciate performers; Encourage and Reward them  
  4701→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4702→
  4703→**Silence**  
  4704→Sometimes when we argue and protest for something, the other person may be adamant, not willing to listen to your logic, and keep on arguing to prove that you are wrong. It happens in the corporate too. Particularly when you argue with your seniors like a senior relative or your boss to put your points in place and the other person does not agree and keep on arguing, you find yourself frustrated wasting unnecessary time. In such times, I have stayed away from such people with no contacts at all for long durations. In the corporate, you can maintain silence to the extent possible except for when you need to discuss or interact. Often I have observed that your silence speaks much louder than words and helps the other person to realize that he was adamant and wrong. I have adopted this policy even with my relatives whenever required.
  4705→
  4706→**Lesson**: Silence is often the best form of protest  
  4707→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4708→
  4709→**Be Respectful**  
  4710→Once my mother rebuked me for no wrong I had done. I gave her back. I did not understand the pain I inflicted on her. I loved her very much and I repented that I was harsh and aggressive. I could have replied in a much respectful manner and not insult her, however, sticking to my point of view.
  4711→
  4712→In a similar manner, I had scolded a colleague very harshly who was much junior in position to me but much senior to me by age. I saw him almost on the verge of collapsing. I learnt one big lesson in life. Even if you have to reprimand someone, particularly higher in age, for something, you can do it in a respectful manner without being harsh. If required, both can share their point of view for better understanding. Even strict punishments can be given as and when necessary, but it has to be in a respectful manner and humanly because older people probably cannot digest insult as easily as they could when they were young. Be polite and respectful to senior people.  That is a sign of good leadership.
  4713→
  4714→**Lesson**: Be respectful; It is a sign of good leadership  
  4715→\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  4716→
  4717→**Whatever happens, it happens for good**  
  4718→Look at so many incidences I narrated before. I met with an accident while coming home from a seminar, but the incident revealed who my true friends were. I was hospitalized after a severe chest pain and underwent angioplasty, but the event taught me to live healthier and realize that Health is Wealth. I did not get promotion for long eight years in my early part of my career, but it helped me to be more perseverant and live with Trust, Hope, Faith and Confidence. When great leaders like the former Tata group Chairman and my MD facilitated me for my success, I learnt what appreciation by humble and caring leaders mean. When seniors accused me wrongly, I learnt how silence can be the best form of protest.
  4719→
  4720→Even when my father and mother died, I thought that it is for their good that their souls would be relieved from the sufferings and pain they were in. It pained me a lot on their demise, but I believed that the truth of death being certain is a creation of the Almighty for something good. Maybe that helped me to become independent from the shelter of my parents and be able to do tough work for which I was dependent on my parents. Loss of your loved one probably provides you greater strength to live on with your life, though at times the emotions of the loss is immeasurable. 
  4721→
  4722→In every undesired event, I learnt something new. Maybe that is the positive way to look at undesirable events. I am not here to prove that every undesirable event will lead to learning something new. But believing in “whatever happens, it happens for good” provides strength to live on with hope of something good lies ahead, triggers new thought process and new learning and helps to tide over such bad or sad uneventful times.
